PixConвҖҷs clean-positiveПҒF=0\rho_{\mathrm{F}}{=}0branch, one switch over UniMatchВ V2, leaves far fewer

Figure 2:The PixCon architecture.PixCon couples two branches over a shared
DINOv2-B encoder and DPT decoder, trained end-to-end under one objective.Consistency branch (top).A weak view of an unlabeled image passes through the EMA
teacher to produce a pseudo-labely^\hat{y}and confidencecc; a strict maskв„і:cвүҘ0.95\mathcal{M}{:}\,c{\geq}0.95filters it, and two CutMix strong views with complementary
channel dropout are trained to agree (в„’u\mathcal{L}_{u}). This branch adopts the weak-to-strong
design of UniMatchВ V2[34].Clean-positive contrastive branch (bottom, ours).The fused decoder feature of eachlabeledimage is projected by a headgП•g_{\phi}to a unit-norm embeddingzz. Theclean-positive filteradmits a pixel as an anchor only when it is labeledandthe student already predicts its label correctly (argвҒЎmaxвҒЎlogits=y\arg\max\,\text{logits}{=}y); admitted
anchors populate the per-class clean-positive bankв„¬k\mathcal{B}_{k}and drive a supervised InfoNCE lossв„’pix\mathcal{L}_{\mathrm{pix}}. Because anchors are guaranteed correct, the bankвҖҷs contamination
rate is zero by construction (ПҒF=0\rho_{\mathrm{F}}{=}0, Sec.3.3), the property that
distinguishes PixCon from confidence-filtered contrastive methods. The two branches share encoder/decoder weights
and are optimised jointly asв„’=12вҖӢ(в„’x+в„’u)+О»pixвҖӢв„’pix\mathcal{L}{=}\tfrac{1}{2}(\mathcal{L}_{x}{+}\mathcal{L}_{u}){+}\lambda_{\mathrm{pix}}\mathcal{L}_{\mathrm{pix}}.

Figure 3:Why clean positives matter.Schematic of the supervised-InfoNCE gradient on
an anchorzaz_{a}of classaa.(a)A confidence-filtered bank (ReCo,
U2PL) admits a small fractionПҒF\rho_{\mathrm{F}}of confidently-wrong
entries, pixels of another classbbenqueued underaa. These act as false positives and
contribute a gradient component (red) pullingzaz_{a}towardthe wrong-class region, so
the net update (black) is deflected.(b)PixCon admits an entry only when it is
labeledandthe student already predicts it correctly, soПҒF=0\rho_{\mathrm{F}}{=}0by
construction and the gradient is pure signal toward the true class-aadensity. At
foundation-model strength the false-positive component would otherwise be the dominant noise
term (Sec.3.3).

Figure 4:Consistent per-epoch margin (3 seeds).EMA-teacher mIoU over training (Pascal,
DINOv2-Base; thin lines per seed, bold the 3-seed mean). At 1/8 (right) the PixCon (green) band sits
above UniMatchВ V2 (blue) across seeds and leads in41/4141/41all-live epochs; at 1/16 (left) the bands
overlap more, matching the smaller+0.39+0.39gain. Beyond the all-live window the means run over fewer
than three live seeds (early stopping) and are not used for the margin claim.

Figure 5:Qualitative wins on PascalВ VOC 1/8.Six per-image PixCon-vs-UniMatchВ V2

ofinput||GT||UniMatchВ V2 repro||PixCon;red contoursoutline disagreement with GT (void ignored; components<0.5%{<}0.5\%suppressed). ADE20K wins are in
Fig.6; the full sixteen-image Pascal and Cityscapes sets are in the

Figure 6:ADE20K 1/8: eight PixCon wins.Per-image PixCon-vs-UniMatchВ V2 comparisons
(150-class palette), ranked by per-image error difference, in the sameinput||GT||UniMatchВ V2||PixConformat as Fig.5;red contoursmark disagreement with GT. Even though the two models tie in aggregate on ADE20K
(+0.13+0.13mIoU, Table3), PixCon replaces UniMatchВ V2вҖҷs large scene-parsing
mislabellings with the correct class, visible per-image gains the aggregate mIoU does not capture.
Two blocks of four; the water-tower example (top right) is the teaser (Fig.1).

header: Appendix BNegative Result: Per-Class Adaptive Thresholds at Foundation-Model Strength

images.caption:
Figure 7:The bottleneck has moved to the encoder, and PixCon adds a lever on top of
it.PascalВ VOC 1/8 mIoU versus total model size (log axis) for representative SSSS methods,
grouped by backbone family (prior-work accuracies from the published numbers; prior-work
parameter counts are standard backbone sizes, approximate, while the DINOv2 sizes24.824.8M/97.597.5M are as reported). Switching a ResNet-101 pipeline to a DINOv2 encoder buys far
more than a decade of method design did at fixed backbone. PixCon (green star) sits at the
DINOv2-B operating point and addsnoappreciable parameters over its UniMatchВ V2-B
baseline, a2Г—1Г—12{\times}1{\times}1projection head and aвүҲ1.4{\approx}1.4MB bank, yet lifts our
compute-matched repro to thereportedUniMatchВ V2-B 1/8 figure (87.9087.90, a+0.89+0.893-seedmeangap that is partly variance reduction; Sec.4.2).
Our runs are compute-matched and below the full UniMatchВ V2 budget, so the green starвҖҷs
position reflects that budget, not a like-for-like comparison with the full-budget dots.

Figure 9:Complete qualitative set: sixteen PixCon wins.All sixteen PascalВ VOC 1/8
validation images with the largest per-image error advantage of PixCon over the UniMatchВ V2
reproduction (DINOv2-Base, EMA-teacher predictions, seedВ 0), as two side-by-side blocks ofinput||ground truth||UniMatchВ V2||PixCon; the bottom strip is
the shared class palette.Red contoursmark connected regions where a prediction
disagrees with ground truth (void ignored; components<0.5%{<}0.5\%of the image suppressed). The
six rows of Fig.5are the top block here. Throughout, the recurring
baseline failure is a part-level class confusion on an already-localised object or a large
spurious region; PixConвҖҷs panels carry far fewer red contours, i.e. it corrects these without
introducing new errors.

Figure 10:Cityscapes qualitative (1/16): near-identical, PixCon marginally cleaner.Six Cityscapes val images with the largest per-image error advantage of PixCon over the
UniMatchВ V2 reproduction (DINOv2-Base, EMA-teacher predictions, seedВ 0), as two side-by-side
blocks ofinput||ground truth||UniMatchВ V2||PixCon; standard
19-class palette, ignore shown white.Red contoursmark connected regions where a
prediction disagrees with ground truth. Consistent with the aggregate tie, the two columns are
nearly identical: PixConвҖҷs advantage here is a handful of small boundary corrections (fewer red
contours on a few building/wall/sidewalk regions), not the large class fixes seen on Pascal.

header: Appendix HTraining Dynamics, Per-Class Breakdown, and Sensitivity

images.caption:
Figure 12:The gain is broad, not rare-class-specific.Per-class IoU change (PixConвҲ’-UniMatchВ V2 reproduction),mean over 3 seedswithВұ\pmstd error bars, on PascalВ VOC 1/16 (left) and 1/8 (right), DINOv2-Base, classes
sorted by baseline difficulty (hardest at top). Green bars are gains, red are regressions.
The net improvement is broad rather than localised, and the largest movers span the frequency
range: at 1/8 (net+0.89+0.89) the biggest gains are tvmonitor+3.9+3.9, sofa+2.9+2.9, chair+2.4+2.4,
car+1.7+1.7; at 1/16 (net+0.39+0.39) sofa+4.6+4.6, chair+2.8+2.8, pottedplant+1.9+1.9, with
regressions (tvmonitorвҲ’4.1-4.1, busвҲ’1.9-1.9). Several per-class deltas carry large seed-to-seed
std (error bars), so we read them as indicative, not individually significant: the evidence
points to a diffuse embedding-space regularisation, not a targeted rare-class fix
(Sec.H.2).
